description.content: Discover energy_sites and other products owned by the authenticated account

Returns a list of products (vehicles and energy_sites) that the authenticated user has access to. Energy sites are identified by an `energy_site_id` and may include Powerwall, Solar, and Megapack assets.
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Static configuration of an energy site (components, address, time zone)
Returns static configuration of the energy site — components installed (Powerwall, Solar, Megapack), address, time zone, version, asset registration metadata, and gateway identifiers.

Historical energy production, consumption, and battery flow

item.name: Tesla Get Energy Site Calendar History

request.name: Tesla Get Energy Site Calendar History

description.content: Returns time-series energy data — production, consumption, grid import/export, battery charge/discharge — aggregated by day, week, month, or year. Supports kind=energy, power, self_consumption, savings, and time_of_use.

Backup reserve threshold for Powerwall sites
Sets the percent state-of-charge that Powerwall holds in reserve for grid outages. Range 0-100. Requires the energy_cmds scope.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Operation mode — self_consumption, backup, or autonomous

Sets the Powerwall operation mode. `self_consumption` prioritizes using stored solar on-site; `backup` reserves battery for outages; `autonomous` lets Tesla optimize against rates and grid conditions.

Reserve threshold for EV charging when the site is islanded from the grid

item.name: Tesla Set Off-Grid Vehicle Charging Reserve

request.name: Tesla Set Off-Grid Vehicle Charging Reserve

description.content: Sets the percent state-of-charge below which Powerwall will stop powering EV charging during a grid outage. Lets owners protect home loads while still allowing some vehicle charging when islanded.

Virtual power plant and grid-services program participation
Returns virtual power plant and grid-services programs the site is eligible for and currently enrolled in (e.g., regional VPP, demand response).
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

The energy_sites surface of the Tesla Fleet API. Lets OAuth-authorized partners and owners read
Powerwall, Solar, and Megapack site state — site info, live status, calendar history, programs —
and write backup reserve, operation mode (self_consumption, backup, autonomous), storm mode,
time-of-use settings, and off-grid vehicle charging reserve.

Authentication uses Tesla Fleet API OAuth 2.0 with the energy_device_data (read) and
energy_cmds (write) scopes. Third-party applications must register a partner account on the
Tesla Developer Portal and pair a virtual key with the owner's account.
